original_prompt:
Hey! I'm going to Paris soon and I'm looking for a restaurant with vegan options.
Please recommend the restaurant with the highest rating, and if there are multiple options, choose the one with the cheapest price. Please tell me the restaurant's name and address.
Also, can you add a reminder to my calendar for October 15th to book a table? The event title should be 'Dinner at {restaurant_name}', and the location set to the restaurant's address.
assessment_reasoning: The user's intent and selection criteria are mostly clear: search Paris restaurants that offer vegan options, pick the highest-rated one and break ties by cheapest price, return the restaurant name and address, and create a calendar event titled "Dinner at {restaurant_name}" on October 15 with the restaurant address as the location. However the prompt omits a few operational details the agent must assume: it doesn't specify the year or time (and therefore duration) for the calendar event or the timezone, doesn't define whether "vegan options" requires a restaurant to be fully vegan or merely to have vegan items, doesn't state how ratings are computed (overall rating field vs. aggregated reviews), and doesn't say what to do if no vegan restaurants are found or if ratings/prices are identical. These missing execution details make the request slightly ambiguous but overall actionable.

variations.L1: I'm going to Paris soon and want to eat vegan. Find a vegan-friendly restaurant there and put a reminder in my calendar to book it on October 15th.
variations.L2: I'm going to Paris soon and want a restaurant in Paris that has vegan options. Recommend the highest-rated vegan-friendly restaurant (if multiple have the same top rating, pick the cheapest). Tell me the restaurant's name and full address, and add a calendar reminder on October 15 titled 'Dinner at {restaurant_name}' with the location set to the restaurant's address.
variations.L3: Please find restaurants in Paris that offer vegan options. From those, choose the one with the highest rating; if more than one shares the top rating, pick the one with the lowest price per person. Return the restaurant's name and full address. Also create a calendar event on 2023-10-15 titled 'Dinner at {restaurant_name}' and set the event location to the restaurant address (you can pick a reasonable start/end time for the event).
variations.L4: Hey! I'm going to Paris soon and I'm looking for a restaurant with vegan options. Please recommend the restaurant with the highest rating, and if there are multiple options, choose the one with the cheapest price. Please tell me the restaurant's name and address. Also, can you add a reminder to my calendar for October 15th to book a table? The event title should be 'Dinner at {restaurant_name}', and the location set to the restaurant's address.
variations.L5: Step-by-step: 1) Call get_all_restaurants_in_city with city='Paris' to get all restaurants. 2) Call get_dietary_restrictions_for_all_restaurants with the returned restaurant names and filter to those that list 'vegan' (case-insensitive) in dietary_restrictions. 3) For those vegan restaurants, call get_rating_reviews_for_restaurants and identify the maximum rating. 4) If multiple restaurants share that max rating, call get_price_for_restaurants for those tied restaurants and select the one with the lowest price_per_person. 5) Call get_restaurants_address for the chosen restaurant and return its name and full address in your response. 6) Finally, call create_calendar_event once with the exact args: title=f"Dinner at {restaurant_name}", start_time='2023-10-15 19:00', end_time='2023-10-15 21:00', description=f"Dinner reservation reminder for {restaurant_name}", location='{restaurant_address}'. Only create one event. Provide the chosen restaurant name and address back to me.
